PAN ROASTED
HALIBUT
WITH CAPERS

Servings: 4 | Prep time: 10 minutes | Cook time: 15 minutes

Ingredients:
- 4 halibut fillets (150g each)
- 3 tablespoons olive oil
- 2 tablespoons capers, rinsed
- 3 cloves garlic, sliced
- 1 lemon, cut into wedges
- 2 tablespoons white wine
- 1 tablespoon butter
- Fresh dill
- Salt and pepper
- 100g baby spinach

Instructions:

1. Pat halibut fillets dry with paper towels.
Season both sides with salt and pepper.

2. Heat olive oil in large skillet over
medium-high heat until shimmering.

3. Place halibut skin-side down in the pan.
Cook for 5 minutes without moving.

4. Carefully flip fillets. Add capers, garlic,
and white wine to the pan.

5. Cook for another 4-5 minutes until the fish
is opaque and flakes easily.

6. Remove fish from pan and set on warm plate.

7. Add spinach to the pan with capers and garlic.
Stir until wilted, about 2 minutes.

8. Add butter and fresh dill. Stir to combine.

9. Plate spinach mixture and top with halibut.
Serve with lemon wedges.

This dish pairs beautifully with white wine
and steamed potatoes.
